stories.headline: April crypto hacks blow past $606M, worst month since Bybit's $1.4B breach
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols have lost more than $606 million across 12 incidents in April, the worst monthly toll since Bybit's $1.4B hack in February 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Bridge and admin-key exploits — not smart-contract bugs — are driving the carnage, signaling DeFi's hardest problem is operational security, not Solidity.
the_details: Kelp DAO lost $292M on April 19 via its LayerZero-powered bridge, draining 116,500 rsETH — about 18% of supply.
Drift Protocol's $285M April 1 exploit is now attributed to North Korea-affiliated actors using pre-signed durable nonces.
Two attacks accounted for 95% of April's losses, with one incident every 2.9 days across 2026.

stories.headline: SEC clears DEX front-ends to operate without broker-dealer registration
stories.tldr: SEC staff said it will not object to software interfaces letting users prepare and submit crypto-asset-security trades without registering as broker-dealers.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The April 13 statement is the clearest signal yet that DeFi front-ends like Uniswap and 1inch can survive in the US without retrofitting TradFi licensing.
the_details: Conditions include not custodying assets, not routing orders for compensation, and clear user disclosures.
The relief sits alongside the SEC-CFTC March 17 joint guidance on a five-category token taxonomy.
Coalition of 100+ crypto firms is still pushing Senate Banking to mark up the CLARITY Act before recess.

stories.headline: Bitcoin hits a wall at $80K as concentrated sells cap a 9-day ETF inflow streak
stories.tldr: BTC failed to break $80,000 despite a nine-day spot-ETF inflow streak topping $2B, with sell-side liquidity concentrated at the round number.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: With FOMC this week and zero cuts priced in, $80K becomes the line that decides whether April closes as BTC's best month in a year or another rejection.
the_details: BTC trades near $78,112, up 0.56%; ETH at $2,328, up 0.61%, both holding key support zones.
Bitcoin-dollar inverse correlation is the most extreme in nearly four years, per CoinDesk.
USDT supply added $5B in the past month, fueling the rebound bid.

stories.headline: Solana ETF inflows collapse 92% to $34M in April from a $419M peak
stories.tldr: Spot Solana ETF inflows have fallen 92% from $419M in November 2025 to just $34M this April, even as SOL trades near $86.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The collapse is the first hard data point that altcoin ETFs do not inherit Bitcoin's structural bid — issuers banking on a copy-paste flywheel are about to learn otherwise.
the_details: SOL is down 32% from its 2025 high; analysts flag $52 as the next major support if $86 fails.
Solana topped Ethereum in weekly dApp revenue for a fifth straight week despite the ETF cooling.
Firedancer is targeting 1M TPS by year-end while Alpenglow has already cut finality below 200ms.

stories.headline: Jupiter unlocks 1.54% of JUP supply worth $9.67M today
stories.tldr: Solana DEX aggregator Jupiter releases 1.54% of circulating JUP supply — about $9.67M — in a scheduled unlock today.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Mid-week unlocks rarely move spot but reset the float math for funds modeling DEX-token supply schedules into Q3.
the_details: Unlock represents one of the larger single-day JUP releases in the current vesting cycle.
Jupiter remains the dominant DEX aggregator on Solana by volume share.
Token unlock follows a quiet stretch for Solana spot flows after November 2025's ETF peak.
